title: Area Converter (m², ft², acre, hectare, tsubo)
shortDescription: Convert between metric, imperial, and Japanese area units. Includes m², km², hectare, ft², acre, mile², and tsubo.
description: Pick a value and unit to see equivalents in every other supported unit. Real estate, land surveys, and construction often mix systems — this calculator unifies them.
metaDescription: Free area converter for square meters, hectares, acres, square feet, square miles, and Japanese tsubo. Exact factors, all-units view, instant browser-based math.

sections.heading: How area conversion works
paragraphs: Each unit pivots through square meters internally. Because length conversions are exact (1 inch = 0.0254 m), every area conversion derived from squaring those factors is also exact. 1 ft² = 0.3048² m² = 0.09290304 m², 1 acre = 4840 yd² = 4046.8564224 m².
Tsubo, the traditional Japanese unit, is 1 ken × 1 ken = 6 shaku × 6 shaku, which works out to about 3.30578 m² (or 1 tsubo ≈ 35.58 ft²). Real estate listings in Japan commonly mix m² and tsubo.

sections.heading: When you'll reach for it
paragraphs: Real estate: floor area in flats and houses, land lot sizes, gardens, parking lots. International buyers regularly need to compare m² with ft²; Japanese buyers compare m² and tsubo simultaneously.

Construction and architecture: material quantities (paint coverage, flooring, tile) are often quoted in one system and used on a project that uses another. Surveying and farming use acres or hectares for land parcels.

sections.heading: Useful reference points
paragraphs: 1 hectare = 10,000 m² = 2.471 acres ≈ a soccer field. 1 acre = 43,560 ft² ≈ 4,047 m². 1 mile² = 640 acres ≈ 2.59 km². 1 tsubo ≈ 3.31 m². A typical Japanese 1LDK apartment is about 30-45 m² (9-14 tsubo). A US single-family lot is often 0.1-0.25 acres (400-1,000 m²).

howTo.name: Enter the area
howTo.text: Type the area value you want to convert.

howTo.name: Choose the source unit
howTo.text: Pick what your value is in.

howTo.name: Compare all units
howTo.text: Every other unit's equivalent appears below.

faq.a: Yes — area factors are derived by squaring exact length factors. The acre is defined as exactly 4046.8564224 m².

faq.q: Why do real estate listings mix units?
faq.a: Different countries grew up with different systems, and locale conventions persist. Japan often shows both m² and tsubo; US mixes ft² and acres.
A traditional Japanese unit equal to roughly two tatami mats — about 3.31 m² or 35.6 ft². Common in Japanese real estate.

faq.q: Hectares vs acres — which is bigger?
Is 1 km² the same as 1 million m²?
faq.a: Yes — 1 km × 1 km = 1,000,000 m². Same for 1 mi² = 27,878,400 ft².
Eight significant digits, more than enough for any practical real estate, construction, or surveying use.

faq.q: Can I use this for paint coverage?
faq.a: Yes. Convert your room's square meters to whatever unit your paint can specifies (often ft²).

faq.q: Does it handle very small areas?
faq.a: Yes — millimeters squared. For microscopy or smaller scales, multiply mm² by 10⁶ to get µm² or 10¹² to get nm².
